name: Acquerello
description: San Francisco Michelin three-star Italian restaurant by Chef Giacomo Artufi, elevating regional Italian cuisine with impeccable technique and premium ingredients. Luxurious tasting menu celebrating Italian culinary heritage.

name: Acquerello Italian Tasting Menu
description: Michelin 3-star Italian fine dining featuring handmade pasta and Piedmont-inspired creations in intimate chef's counter and private room.

name: Alcatraz Island Ferry Tour
description: Historic former federal penitentiary accessible only by official audio tour; cellhouse remains hauntingly preserved with stories of famous inmates and daring escape attempts.

name: Allied Arts Guild Cafe
description: Charming garden cafe in a Spanish Colonial courtyard with sandwiches, salads, and coffee. Shaded seating under umbrellas surrounded by artist studios and galleries.

name: Allied Arts Guild
description: Spanish Colonial-style compound with artist studios, galleries, craft shops, and charming courtyard. Watch artisans create pottery, jewelry, and textiles in working studios.

name: Almaden Quicksilver County Park
description: 1,366-acre park with 22 miles of hiking trails, mining history, scenic overlooks, and wildlife viewing. Rich in both nature and heritage.

name: Anderson Collection at Stanford
description: Intimate museum featuring American modern and contemporary art with works by Diebenkorn, Elsworth Kelly, and Serra. Elegant modernist building designed by Renzo Piano.

name: Andytown Coffee Roasters
description: Specialty coffee roaster in Mission with espresso-focused culture; single-origin beans and knowledgeable baristas serving exacting standards.

name: Año Nuevo Elephant Seals
description: Remote island sanctuary hosting massive northern elephant seals in their natural habitat. Guided walks reveal these magnificent creatures hauling out and breeding on pristine beaches.

name: Aphotic
description: San Francisco experimental Michelin-starred restaurant serving cuisine in complete darkness with multi-sensory focus on taste, smell, sound, and touch. Unique culinary adventure challenging conventional fine dining expectations.

name: Apple Park Visitor Center
description: Marvel at Apple's iconic ring-shaped headquarters and browse the curated design-focused Apple Store with exclusive tech products and merchandise.

name: Aquarium of the Bay
description: 3-story aquarium at Fisherman's Wharf featuring 20,000+ marine animals in 700,000 gallons; sea otters, jellyfish, and interactive touch pools.
address: Pier 39, Building A, San Francisco, CA 94109

name: Atelier Crenn
description: San Francisco's Michelin three-star restaurant celebrating Chef Dominique Crenn's artistry with abstract French cuisine, plated as edible paintings with poetic influences. Destination fine dining of incomparable creativity.

name: Atelier Crenn Poetic Tasting
description: Michelin 3-star restaurant with abstract plated cuisine; chef Dominique Crenn's 'poetic culinaire' philosophy in intimate setting.

name: Bar Crenn Avant-Garde Cocktails
description: Michelin-starred cocktail bar by chef Crenn with poetic mixology; artistic beverage presentations and theatrical service in minimalist space.

name: Bedwell Bayfront Park
description: Restored tidal marsh with 2.5-mile walking trail, scenic bay views, and prime birding. Restored wetlands supporting salt marsh harvest mouse and endangered plants.

name: Benu
description: San Francisco Michelin-starred laboratory of Chef Corey Lee's culinary ideas, featuring playful high-concept dishes blending Korean, French, and California influences with meticulous execution. Creative tasting menu experience.

name: Beringer Vineyards
description: Historic 1875 winery in St. Helena featuring the famous Rhine House, limestone caverns, and extensive wine library spanning multiple centuries. Premier Napa destination combining heritage architecture with world-class Cabernet productions.

name: Bi-Rite Creamery
description: Seasonal ice cream made fresh daily from local ingredients; quirky flavors like sage panna cotta and Mission district favorite since 2006.

name: Big Basin Redwoods State Park
description: Majestic old-growth redwood forest with waterfalls, ancient groves, and pristine wilderness. California's oldest state park offering diverse trails from easy nature walks to challenging summits.
address: 21600 Big Basin Way, Boulder Creek, CA 95006

name: Bing Concert Hall
description: World-class 750-seat concert venue hosting Stanford Symphony, classical ensembles, and guest performers. Intimate acoustic design with cutting-edge building materials.

name: Bird Dog
description: Contemporary American gastropub with craft cocktails, seasonal small plates, and a lively bar scene. Industrial-meets-rustic aesthetic with open kitchen and local artwork.

name: Birdsong
description: San Francisco Michelin-starred restaurant by Chef/Owner Evan Funke (also Lazy Bear), serving precise French-inflected cuisine at an intimate 12-seat chef's counter with theatrical presentation. Exceptional multi-course tasting menu.

name: Bistro Vida
description: Intimate French bistro with charcuterie boards, braised meats, and French wine list. Exposed brick, candlelit tables, and a warm atmosphere perfect for date nights.

name: Blue Bottle Coffee Mint Plaza
description: Flagship cafe in SoMa's quiet plaza with single-origin pour-overs, Japanese-style precision, and minimalist aesthetic.

name: Bonny Doon Vineyard
description: Eccentric and innovative Santa Cruz Mountains winery known for creative blends and quirky labels. Intimate tasting room with knowledgeable staff and dramatic vineyard views.
address: 2010 Bonny Doon Rd, Santa Cruz, CA 95060

name: Bryant Street (Palo Alto)
description: Eclectic mixed-use street with craft cocktail bars, wine shops, indie bookstores, and niche retailers. Tree-lined sidewalks with a bohemian, artistic neighborhood vibe.

name: Burgess Park
description: Scenic 40-acre park with wetland preserve, fishing pond, picnic areas, and nature trails. Birdwatching hotspot with herons, egrets, and seasonal migrant species.

name: Burma Superstar Shan Noodles
description: Popular Burmese restaurant famous for shan noodles with crispy noodles, fish broth, and lime; casual Mission location always packed.

name: Cakebread Cellars
description: Family-owned Rutherford estate producing benchmark Cabernet Sauvignon and Chardonnay with meticulous attention to terroir, opulence, and balance. Welcoming tastings celebrate California's winemaking excellence.

name: Calafia Cafe
description: Mediterranean-inspired cafe with creamy lattes, fresh pastries, and light lunch plates. Warm, inviting space with artwork, outdoor seating, and a neighborhood feel.

name: California Academy of Sciences
description: Natural history museum with living roof, aquarium, planetarium, and rainforest dome; hands-on exhibits for all ages in Golden Gate Park.
address: 55 Music Concourse Drive, San Francisco, CA 94118

name: Cantor Arts Center
description: World-class art museum with medieval sculptures, Old Masters, contemporary installations, and rotating exhibitions across 24 galleries. Free admission.

name: Castello di Amorosa
description: Breathtaking 121,000 sq ft Italian castle reproduction in Napa Valley's premier tasting destination with sweeping vineyard vistas. Underground caves and multiple tasting rooms provide an immersive medieval wine experience.
address: 4045 St. Helena Highway North, Calistoga, CA 94515

name: Castle Rock State Park
description: Dramatic sandstone formations and scenic ridgeline with sweeping bay views. Castle Rock summit offers stunning vistas and excellent hiking on well-maintained trails.
address: 15000 Page Mill Road, Los Altos Hills, CA 94022

name: Castro Theatre Historic Cinema
description: 1922 Spanish Baroque movie palace with ornate red velvet and gilt interior; catch classic films and silents on the Mighty Wurlitzer organ.

name: Chez Panisse
description: Berkeley Michelin-starred legend founded by Chef Alice Waters, pioneering farm-to-table movement with seasonal menus and passionate local sourcing. Cultural institution celebrating California's culinary revolution.

name: Chez TJ
description: Mountain View Michelin-starred restaurant delivering progressive California French cuisine with artful presentation and impeccable service. Sophisticated tasting menu in intimate neighborhood setting.

name: Children's Discovery Museum of San Jose
description: Interactive play-based learning museum with 150+ hands-on exhibits for young explorers. Splash areas, ride-on vehicles, and creative studios.

name: Chinatown Dim Sum & Exploration
description: Historic 150-year-old neighborhood dense with Grant Avenue's dragon lanterns, dim sum parlors, herb shops, and Tin How Temple accessible via narrow alleys.

name: Coal Creek Preserve
description: Quiet redwood preserve in remote canyon with pristine creek and mossy forest. Minimal crowds and excellent for peaceful forest immersion off main trails.
address: Coal Creek Road, Palo Alto, CA 94301

name: Coit Tower & Summit Views
description: 1933 Art Deco tower atop Telegraph Hill with murals, 360-degree city vistas, and elevator to crown level; surrounded by charming Telegraph Hill neighborhood.
address: 1 Telegraph Hill Boulevard, San Francisco, CA 94133

name: Computer History Museum
description: Explore 75+ years of computing innovation through interactive exhibits and rare artifacts. Journey from mechanical calculators to modern AI.
address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043

name: Conservatory of Flowers
description: 1879 Victorian glass greenhouse with palm jungle, orchids, carnivorous plants, and tropical exhibits in Golden Gate Park.
address: 100 John F Kennedy Drive, San Francisco, CA 94118

name: Coupa Cafe
description: Legendary open-air spot on Stanford campus where tech founders brainstorm over artisan coffee and fresh pastries. Intimate tables, free WiFi, and the unofficial startup networking hub.

name: Crissy Field Beach & Promenade
description: Former airfield transformed into sandy beach with unobstructed Golden Gate Bridge views, picnic areas, and direct water access for windsurfing.

name: Crystal Springs Trail
description: Scenic 10-mile reservoir trail with forest and oak woodland alongside pristine water. Rolling terrain perfect for day hiking with peaceful bay views from high points.
address: Crystal Springs Road, San Mateo County, CA 94401

name: Cupertino Library Reading & Study Space
description: Modern, light-filled library with quiet reading areas, tech resources, and community spaces. Beautiful architecture perfect for studying.

name: de Young Museum
description: Copper-clad art museum in Golden Gate Park displaying American art, textiles, and international works with a 360-degree observation tower.
address: 50 Hagiwara Tea Garden Drive, San Francisco, CA 94118

name: Devil's Slide Trail
description: Spectacular 6-mile coastal bluff trail with 1,000-foot drops and panoramic ocean views. Dramatic landscape carved by legendary landslides with wildflowers blooming spring through summer.

name: Dolores Park Picnic & Views
description: 14-acre valley park with wide lawns, Twin Peaks backdrop, and weekend scene; perfect for picnicking, people-watching, and sunbathing.

name: Domaine Carneros
description: Champagne-style sparkling wines in a stunning château overlooking the Carneros region, with elegant seated tastings on the terrace. Premium production facility where you can watch bottles age in traditional méthode champenoise style.

name: Domaine Chandon
description: Elegant sparkling wine estate in Yountville crafted by the French Moët & Chandon house, with beautiful tasting rooms and manicured gardens. Experience méthode champenoise wines in a refined California setting.

name: Duarte's Tavern
description: Legendary 1894 saloon and restaurant famous for artichoke soup and fresh seafood. Historic watering hole with old-world charm and coastal hospitality.

name: Edgewood Park and Preserve
description: Rare serpentine grassland preserve with stunning wildflower displays and native plants. Spring transforms hillsides into colorful quilts of Indian paintbrush and lupine.
address: Mountain Home Road, Redwood City, CA 94062

name: El Corte de Madera Creek Preserve
description: Remote redwood preserve with pristine creek and old-growth forest. Excellent destination for solitude and nature immersion with minimal visitor impact.

name: El Farolito 24H Burritos
description: 24-hour Mission fixture with massive flour burritos, late-night crowds, and minimal frills; perfect for post-bar carbs or early morning.

name: Evvia Estiatorio
description: Upscale Mediterranean restaurant with wood-fired pasta, fresh seafood, and wine cellar showcasing Italian and Greek vintages. Open kitchen with marble countertops.

name: Exploratorium
description: Interactive science museum at Pier 15 with 650+ hands-on exhibits exploring light, sound, motion, and the human experience with a real fog bridge.

name: Far Niente
description: Oakville estate since 1885 producing Cabernet Sauvignon and Chardonnay of legendary status, featuring magnificent limestone wine cave and manicured grounds. Sophisticated tastings available by appointment only.

name: Ferry Building Marketplace
description: Historic 1898 Beaux-Arts terminal transformed into gourmet food hall with artisan vendors, farmers market (Tues/Thurs/Sat), and bay-view dining.

name: Filoli Historic House & Gardens
description: 16,000-seat Georgian Revival mansion with 16 acres of formal gardens featuring rose gardens, sunken gardens, and manicured hedgerows. Seasonal tours and garden walks.

name: Fisherman's Wharf & Waterfront
description: Working waterfront district with seafood restaurants, sea lion colonies, fishing boats, Pier 39 shops, and sourdough bread bowls.

name: Foothills Park
description: Premier valley-view preserve with oak woodland and scenic trail system. 1,500-acre park offering peaceful hiking with panoramic Santa Clara Valley vistas.
address: 3000 Page Mill Road, Palo Alto, CA 94306

name: Frog's Leap Winery
description: Sustainable Napa Valley icon pioneering organic and biodynamic viticulture since 1981, known for elegant Zinfandel and Cabernet Sauvignon. Charming redwood barn tasting room reflects the winery's quirky, wine-first philosophy.

name: Golden Gate Bridge Walk
description: Iconic 1.7-mile pedestrian crossing of the world-famous International Orange suspension bridge with panoramic Bay views and Marin Headlands access.
address: Golden Gate Bridge, San Francisco, CA 94129

name: Golden Gate Park (East Section) Exploration
description: 1000-acre urban park's eastern section featuring gardens, museums, and meadows; walk from Panhandle to Conservatory Valley with museums.
address: Golden Gate Park, San Francisco, CA 94118

name: Googleplex Outdoor Sculpture Loop
description: Walk Google's picturesque campus to view the Android statue garden, colorful sculptures, and innovative architecture. Self-guided free access.

name: Haight-Ashbury Counterculture Shopping
description: Historic 1960s hippie district dense with vintage clothing, record shops, psychedelic murals, and Ben & Jerry's; vibrant street culture and restaurants.

name: Half Moon Bay Distillery
description: Craft distillery producing small-batch spirits with local ingredients and character. Tasting room and tours showcase artisanal techniques and creative spirits.
address: 131 Stone Pine Rd, Half Moon Bay, CA 94019

name: Half Moon Bay State Beach
description: Wide sandy beach framed by dramatic sea cliffs, perfect for sunset walks and tide pool exploration. Pristine Pacific shoreline with gentle waves ideal for families.

name: Harley Farms Goat Dairy
description: Working goat dairy farm offering artisanal cheese tastings and farm tours. Watch cheesemakers at work and taste award-winning cheeses in rustic farm setting.

name: Hayes Valley Shopping & Dining
description: Charming neighborhood west of Civic Center with boutique shops, design studios, independent restaurants, and café culture.

name: San Jose Heritage Rose Garden
description: Beautiful rose garden with 3,500 plants and nearly 3,000 rare varieties. Scenic riverside location perfect for peaceful strolls and photography.
address: Spring St & Guadalupe River Park, San Jose, CA 95110

name: Hess Collection Winery
description: Mount Veeder estate combining world-class Cabernet Sauvignon with impressive contemporary art collection and dramatic mountain vineyard views. Modern tasting rooms provide refined wine education experiences.

name: Hicklebee's Children's Bookstore
description: Independent children's bookstore with curated selection, story times, and knowledgeable staff. Community hub for young readers and families.

name: History Park at Kelley Park
description: Historic village with period buildings, museums, and exhibitions showcasing Santa Clara Valley's rich cultural heritage and pioneer history.

name: Hoover Tower
description: A 285-foot sandstone tower offering panoramic views of Silicon Valley, the bay, and distant mountains from the observation deck. Museum exhibits chronicle early 20th-century American history.

name: Inglenook
description: Historic 1887 Napa Valley estate and film museum owned by Francis Ford Coppola, blending wine heritage with cinematic artistry. Exclusive wine tastings in restored Victorian buildings with views of the original vineyard plantings.

name: Intel Museum
description: Discover computing's evolution through Intel's perspective, featuring rare processors, memory chips, and interactive demonstrations of chip technology.
address: 2200 Mission College Blvd, Santa Clara, CA 95054

name: Iron Horse Vineyards
description: Sonoma's premier sparkling wine estate in the Green Valley with stunning views across 150 acres of vineyards toward San Francisco Bay. Relaxed outdoor tastings celebrate exceptional Chardonnay-based productions.

name: Japanese Tea Garden
description: 19-acre serene garden with arched bridges, stone lanterns, moon gates, and traditional tea house serving matcha in Golden Gate Park.
address: 75 Hagiwara Tea Garden Drive, San Francisco, CA 94118

name: Japantown San Jose
description: Historic cultural district with Japanese restaurants, shops, theaters, and community events. Sunday farmers market and traditional festivals.
address: Jackson Street between 3rd & 6th Streets, San Jose, CA 95112

name: Junior Museum & Zoo (Palo Alto)
description: Family-friendly museum with interactive nature exhibits, small-animal zoo featuring mountain lions, owls, and snakes, plus nature trails and creek exploration.

name: La Honda Creek Open Space Preserve
description: Secluded redwood canyon with mossy creek flowing through old-growth forest. Peaceful preserve with minimal crowds perfect for nature immersion and bird watching.

name: La Strada
description: Stylish Italian trattoria with handmade pasta, risotto, and tiramisu. Warm lighting, linen tablecloths, and an inviting wine bar with an extensive list of Italian bottles.

name: La Taqueria Classic Tacos
description: Mission institution with grilled meats, hand-pressed tortillas, and straightforward taco excellence; no-frills counter service since 1994.

name: Lands End Coastal Trail
description: Dramatic 3.3-mile clifftop trail tracing SF's western edge with sea stacks, tide pools, and coastal vistas; accessible via Visitor Center.
address: 680 Point Lobos Avenue, San Francisco, CA 94121

name: Lazy Bear
description: San Francisco neighborhood Michelin-starred restaurant showcasing inventive seasonal American cuisine with energetic open kitchen and convivial atmosphere. Chef Evan Funke's creative tasting menus in intimate wine-forward setting.

name: Levi's Stadium Tours
description: Behind-the-scenes tours of the 49ers' home stadium including suites, field access, and 49ers Museum. Seasonal tour availability.
address: 4900 Marie P Debartolo Way, Santa Clara, CA 95054

name: Lick Observatory
description: Historic mountaintop observatory with spectacular views and educational programs. Telescopes, visitor center, and hiking trails on Mount Hamilton.
address: 7281 Mt Hamilton Rd, Mount Hamilton, CA 95140

name: Liholiho Yacht Club Hawaiian Fusion
description: Michelin-starred Hawaiian cuisine blending island traditions with California ingredients; chef Ravi Kapur's playful modern approach.

name: Linda Mar Beach
description: Sheltered sandy cove backed by sandy dunes and coastal bluffs in Pacifica. Excellent for swimming, picnicking, and spotting migrating gray whales in winter months.

name: Lombard Street Crooked Mile
description: World's crookiest street with eight hairpin turns; walk down Russian Hill's famous S-curve lined with hydrangea gardens and photo-op ready switchbacks.

name: Long Ridge Preserve
description: Expansive ridge-top preserve with open grasslands and oak woodlands offering panoramic views. Multiple trail loops with wildflower displays and peaceful ridgeline hiking.
address: Page Mill Road, Palo Alto, CA 94301

name: MacArthur Park
description: Upscale contemporary American steakhouse with dry-aged beef, whiskey selection, and dim lighting. Classic cocktails and attentive service in an intimate setting.

name: MACLA Gallery (Movimiento de Arte y Cultura Latinoamericana)
description: Vibrant Latinx art space with rotating exhibitions, performances, and cultural programs celebrating Latino and immigrant artists.

name: Madera at Rosewood
description: Menlo Park Michelin-starred restaurant by Chef Andrew Soto, featuring refined California cuisine with French techniques and elegant plating in sophisticated contemporary setting. Fine dining in luxury hotel environment.
address: 2241 Sand Hill Road, Menlo Park, CA 94025

name: Madera at Rosewood
description: Fine-dining farmhouse restaurant inside Rosewood Sand Hill resort with farm-to-table cuisine, wine-focused menus, and rustic-elegant ambiance overlooking gardens.
address: 2241 Junipero Serra Boulevard, Palo Alto, CA 94306

name: Manresa
description: Los Gatos Michelin-starred temple of haute cuisine by Chef David Kinch, pioneering ingredient-obsessed tasting menu with artistic plating and innovative techniques. Extraordinary multi-sensory culinary experience.

name: Mavericks Beach Viewpoint
description: Legendary big wave surfing spot with dramatic cliff views and churning Pacific swells. Watch world-class surfers tackle massive waves from the safe vantage point above.
address: Pillar Point, Half Moon Bay, CA 94019

name: Memorial Park
description: Beautiful redwood forest along Pescadero Creek with waterfalls and old-growth groves. Excellent base for family camping with easy creek-side and trail hiking.

name: Mission District Burrito Crawl
description: Self-guided food tour through the Mission's legendary taquerias including La Taqueria, El Farolito, and Mission favorites, ending at Bi-Rite Creamery.

name: Mission Cheese Shop
description: Artisan cheese monger curating global selections; knowledgeable staff provide tastings and pairings with adjacent wine bar.

name: Mission Dolores Basilica
description: 1776 Spanish mission church, SF's oldest building, with original wooden beams, cemetery, and museum documenting California's founding.

name: Montara Lighthouse
description: Historic 1875 lighthouse situated on dramatic coastal bluff accessible via scenic coastal trail. Youth hostel today with restored keeper's quarters and stunning sunset vistas.

name: Mori Point Trail
description: Scenic 3-mile loop through coastal scrub and grasslands with panoramic ocean views and native wildflowers. Excellent for birdwatching and spotting butterflies and sea lions.

name: Downtown Mountain View - Castro Street
description: Charming seven-block pedestrian district with shops, restaurants, and cafes. Vibrant small-town main street energy in Silicon Valley's heart.
address: Castro Street between El Camino & Central Expressway, Mountain View, CA 94041

name: Mountain View Farmers Market
description: Year-round Sunday market featuring fresh local produce, artisanal goods, and prepared foods. Thursday evening market May-September.
address: 600 W Evelyn Ave, Mountain View, CA 94041

name: Mourad
description: San Francisco Michelin-starred restaurant celebrating North African and Mediterranean cuisine by Chef Mourad Lahlou, featuring vibrant spices, rustic elegance, and theatrical presentation. Aromatic culinary journey.
address: 140 New Montgomery Street, San Francisco, CA 94105

name: Muir Woods National Monument
description: Cathedral-like old-growth redwood forest with towering ancient trees and misty groves. Magical destination just north of Golden Gate Bridge with accessible trails for all abilities.
address: 1 Muir Woods Rd, Mill Valley, CA 94941

name: NASA Ames Exploration Center
description: Explore space science through NASA exhibits, simulators, and educational displays at Moffett Field's free public visitor center.
address: Building 19, Moffett Field, Mountain View, CA 94035

name: North Beach Espresso & Italian Culture
description: Historic Italian neighborhood (City Lights Books, Caffè Trieste) dense with 1950s Beat poetry spirit, narrow alleys, and traditional Italian bakeries.

name: Ocean Beach & Great Highway
description: 1.3-mile sandy Pacific shore with waves, occasional seals, windswept dunes, and the Great Highway promenade perfect for sunset walks.

name: Opus One
description: Ultra-premium Napa Valley Bordeaux blend producer housed in stunning semicircular architecture, with world-class art collection and barrel room views. Exclusive appointment-only tastings celebrate architectural mastery and exceptional wines.

name: Oren's Hummus
description: Israeli fast-casual with creamy hummus plates, falafel, and fresh pita. Bright, minimalist interior with orders served in clay bowls on rustic wooden tables.

name: Pacifica Pier
description: Iconic wooden pier extending into the Pacific with fishing opportunities and sweeping ocean vistas. Perfect for sunset strolls and spotting sea lions and seabirds.

name: Painted Ladies Victorian Row
description: Seven iconic pastel Victorians on Hayes Street facing Alamo Square park; perfectly restored 1892 mansions backed by downtown SF skyline.

name: Palace of Fine Arts
description: Neoclassical Beaux-Arts palace from 1915 Panama-Pacific Expo with grand dome reflected in lagoon; walkable grounds perfect for photos and relaxation.

name: Palo Alto Baylands Park
description: 1,000+ acre preserve with hiking/biking trails, boardwalks through tidal marshes, and visitor center with educational exhibits. Excellent for birding and wildlife viewing.
address: 2500 East Bayshore Road, Palo Alto, CA 94303

name: Pescadero Creek Park
description: Scenic redwood park with creek swimming holes and moss-covered forest trails. Serene destination for wading, picnicking, and enjoying pristine watershed.
address: Pescadero Creek Road, Loma Mar, CA 94021

name: Pescadero Marsh
description: Pristine estuary and natural preserve teeming with waterfowl, egrets, and herons. Boardwalk and trail system perfect for bird enthusiasts and nature photographers.
address: Pescadero Marsh Nature Preserve, Pescadero, CA 94060

name: Pescadero Town
description: Charming coastal village with vintage shops, art galleries, and local character. Historic buildings and community spots perfect for leisurely browsing and discovering local crafts.

name: Phil's Coffee
description: Third-wave specialty coffee roaster in a minimalist space with single-origin espresso, pour-overs, and homemade pastries. Local artists rotate wall installations.

name: Phleger Estate
description: Pristine old-growth redwood forest and oak woodland on 1,000-acre estate. Quiet preserve with excellent trails through ancient groves and creek canyons.
address: Sulfer Spring Road, Palo Alto, CA 94301

name: Pier 39 Sea Lions
description: Hundreds of California sea lions lounging on wooden docks at Pier 39's west side; free viewing platform with marine interpretation.

name: Pigeon Point Lighthouse
description: Stunning 115-foot white lighthouse dramatically perched on coastal bluffs with waves crashing below. Historic 1872 sentinel offering guided tours and breathtaking ocean panoramas.

name: Pillar Point Bluff Trail
description: Dramatic clifftop trail with views of Mavericks break and offshore rocks. Excellent for whale watching in winter and stunning sunset photography any season.
address: Pillar Point, Half Moon Bay, CA 94019

name: Pillar Point Harbor
description: Working fishing harbor with charter boats, fish markets, and coastal charm. Watch fishing vessels return at dusk with the day's catch and explore seaside shops.

name: Pizzeria Delfina (Palo Alto)
description: Neapolitan-style pizzeria with wood-fired oven, San Marzano tomatoes, and imported mozzarella. Simple menu, natural wines, and communal seating create a casual Italian vibe.

name: Plaza de César Chávez
description: Downtown civic plaza with iconic fountains, splash pad, shaded seating, and community gathering space. Site of cultural events and celebrations.
address: 170 S Market St, San Jose, CA 95113

name: Pomponio State Beach
description: Secluded sandy beach with dramatic sandstone cliffs and pristine shoreline. One of the Coast's least-crowded gems perfect for solitude and coastal beauty.

name: Powell-Hyde Cable Car Ride
description: Historic cable car line climbing Nob Hill's steepest grades with views of the Bay; board downtown and enjoy vintage wooden car experience.
address: Powell & Market Street, San Francisco, CA 94102

name: Purisima Creek Redwoods Preserve
description: Hidden gem featuring lush redwood groves and mossy creek corridors with ferns and wildflowers. Quiet 8-mile loop through pristine forest rarely crowded.
address: Higgins Purisima Road, Half Moon Bay, CA 94019

name: Quince
description: San Francisco Michelin-starred restaurant by Chef Michael Tusk showcasing Italy-inspired cuisine with pristine seasonal ingredients and elegant technique. Refined tasting menu celebrating California's terroir and Mediterranean traditions.

name: Quince Italian Fine Dining
description: Michelin-starred Italian cuisine near Ferry Plaza; chef Evan Funke's handmade pasta and ingredient-focused preparations in refined setting.

name: Rancho San Antonio Preserve
description: Expansive oak and chaparral preserve with creek canyons and mountain vistas. Extensive trail network ranging from easy creek walks to challenging ridge climbs.
address: 23525 Foothill Road, Los Altos Hills, CA 94022

name: Reposado
description: Upscale Mexican restaurant featuring fresh ceviches, wood-fired dishes, and premium tequila/mezcal selection. Modern interiors with cactus gardens and warm lighting.

name: Reposado Happy Hour
description: Afternoon/evening drink specials with discounted tequila cocktails, beer, and wine plus appetizers at Reposado. Social scene with views of downtown Palo Alto.

name: Ridge Vineyards Monte Bello
description: Legendary Santa Cruz Mountains estate at 2,600ft elevation producing iconic Monte Bello Cabernet Blend with panoramic Bay Area vistas. Rustic winery building offers intimate tastings with breathtaking scenic backdrop.

name: Robert Mondavi Winery
description: Iconic Napa Valley vineyard pioneering American fine wine, featuring architectural masterpiece designed by A.E. Morris with oak-lined corridors and terrace vistas. Signature Cabernet Sauvignon tastes in refined settings.

name: Rodin Sculpture Garden at Stanford
description: Serene garden featuring 20+ original Rodin bronzes including The Thinker and The Gates of Hell, set among native oaks and stone sculptures. Free and open year-round.

name: Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum
description: Largest Egyptian artifact collection west of the Rockies featuring mummies, sarcophagi, and ancient treasures in an atmospheric neo-Egyptian setting.

name: Russian Ridge Preserve
description: Iconic ridgeline preserve with rolling grasslands and sweeping valley-to-bay views. Wind farm area with excellent wildflower displays and peaceful scenic trails.
address: Page Mill Road, Palo Alto, CA 94301

name: Saison
description: San Francisco Michelin three-star culinary innovation epicenter by Chef Joshua Skenes, delivering avant-garde tasting menus with theatrical presentation and boundary-pushing techniques. Immersive gastronomic theatre.

name: Saison Experimental Omakase
description: Michelin 3-star Japanese-California fusion; chef Joshua Skenes' 20+ course tasting with open kitchen fire and avant-garde techniques.

name: Sam McDonald Park
description: Hidden redwood forest gem featuring ancient groves, Pescadero Creek, and 12 miles of quiet trails. Excellent for backpacking with rustic campsites beneath towering trees.

name: Sam's Chowder House
description: Casual coastal restaurant serving fresh seafood and creamy New England chowder with bay views. Quintessential Half Moon Bay dining experience with friendly atmosphere.
address: 4210 N Cabrillo Hwy, Half Moon Bay, CA 94019

name: San Gregorio Beach
description: Wide sandy beach tucked into secluded cove with sandstone cliffs and fresh water creek. Pristine and less crowded than neighboring beaches with excellent tide pool exploration.

name: San Jose Museum of Art
description: Contemporary art museum featuring California artists and rotating international exhibitions. Beautiful downtown location with free Thursday nights.
address: 110 S Market St, San Jose, CA 95113

name: San Pedro Square Market
description: Vibrant downtown food and beverage hall with 20+ vendors, craft cocktails, and local cuisine. Dynamic gathering space with live events.
address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110

name: Sand Hill Road VC Tour
description: Historic venture capital corridor with glass-walled headquarters of Sequoia Capital, Kleiner Perkins, and Index Ventures. Self-guided walking tour through the heart of Silicon Valley's investment power.
address: Sand Hill Road, Menlo Park, CA 94025

name: Santana Row
description: Upscale mixed-use complex with 50+ shops, 30 restaurants, spas, boutique hotel, and cinema. Urban plaza with year-round events.

name: Saratoga Hakone Estate and Gardens
description: Serene Japanese gardens with traditional architecture, bamboo groves, and scenic views. Peaceful retreat with tea house and koi ponds.

name: Schramsberg Vineyards
description: Premier Napa sparkling wine producer with 200 acres of vineyards and fascinating 19-acre underground cavern cellars carved from volcanic rock. Expert-led cave tours pair with premium méthode champenoise tastings.

name: San Francisco Museum of Modern Art
description: 7-story striking design museum featuring Rothkos, Basquiats, photography, and cutting-edge contemporary installations in a prime SoMa location.

name: Shoreline Lake Boating
description: Rent kayaks, paddleboats, or sailboats on serene Shoreline Lake surrounded by parkland. Perfect for peaceful water recreation and scenic views.
address: 3160 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043

name: Sightglass Coffee Roastery
description: Industrial SoMa coffee roastery with high ceilings, roasting visible through glass, and premium espresso; design-forward counter space.

name: Single Thread
description: Healdsburg Michelin-starred restaurant with hyper-seasonal Japanese-influenced California cuisine, featuring only 12 seats in an intimate chef's counter setting. Exceptionally precise multi-course tasting menu showcasing local terroir.

name: Skyline-to-the-Sea Trail
description: Epic 10-mile one-way journey from mountain ridge to pristine Beach Camp along creek and redwood groves. Legendary Bay Area backpacking route through old-growth forest.
address: Big Basin State Park, Boulder Creek, CA 95006

name: Slanted Door Vietnamese Fine Dining
description: Michelin-starred Vietnamese restaurant at Ferry Building with floor-to-ceiling bay windows; modern takes on pho, banh mi, and fresh preparations.

name: Smitten Ice Cream Liquid Nitrogen
description: Theatrical liquid-nitrogen ice cream made to order in front of you; scientific approach with premium local dairy and daily flavor rotations.
address: Ferry Building Marketplace, San Francisco, CA 94111

name: Sons & Daughters
description: San Francisco Michelin-starred intimate dining featuring daily-changing menus by Chefs Rich Shih and Stephanie Izard, emphasizing impeccable ingredient quality and precise technique. Counter seating for immersive tasting experience.

name: Spruce
description: San Francisco Michelin-starred restaurant in Pacific Heights featuring refined French-California cuisine with impeccable service and elegant wood-accented interior. Classic fine dining with seasonal tasting options.

name: Stags' Leap Winery
description: Storied Napa Valley winery with 60+ years of Cabernet Sauvignon heritage, featuring dramatic waterfall and natural amphitheater setting. Estate hospitality centers on legendary wines and stunning grounds.

name: Stanford Campus Walking Tour
description: Explore the vast 8,180-acre campus with Spanish Romanesque architecture, Palm Drive, and the iconic Main Quad. Self-guided or join a docent-led tour to discover tech history and academic landmarks.

name: Stanford Dish Loop Hike
description: Iconic 3.3-mile loop trail with wildflower meadows, oak groves, and panoramic views of the Bay. Popular sunrise/sunset spot featuring a large radio telescope and rolling grasslands.

name: Stanford Memorial Church
description: Stunning Romanesque church with intricate mosaics, stained glass windows, and soaring arches. Built in 1903 as a memorial to Jane Stanford; offers architectural tours and Sunday services.

name: Stanford Shopping Center
description: Outdoor mall with upscale retailers like Nordstrom, Macy's, and designer boutiques. Tree-lined walkways, open-air courtyards, and seasonal events.
address: 660 Stanford Shopping Center, Palo Alto, CA 94304

name: Stanford Stadium Tour
description: Historic 50,000-capacity stadium where the Stanford Cardinal play football, offering tours of locker rooms, field views, and sports memorabilia showcasing 125+ years of tradition.

name: State Bird Provisions Dim Sum
description: Innovative dim sum restaurant serving small plates via Japanese-style cart; creative fusion approach to traditional Cantonese tea service.

name: Sterling Vineyards
description: Stunning hillside castle overlooking Napa Valley at 1,100ft elevation with cable car access and panoramic vineyard vistas, producing elegant Cabernet Sauvignon and Chardonnay. Self-guided tasting experience with unforgettable views.

name: Stevens Creek County Park
description: 1,042-acre park with scenic reservoir, hiking trails, and oak woodland. Popular for outdoor recreation, wildlife viewing, and picnicking.

name: Stow Lake Pedal Boating
description: Scenic 45-acre lake in Golden Gate Park's central valley with rental pedal boats, Dragon's Gate bridge, and island hikes.
address: Stow Lake Drive East, San Francisco, CA 94118

name: Sunnyvale Heritage Park Museum
description: Local history museum housed in period buildings showcasing Sunnyvale's transformation from orchard land to tech hub with artifacts and exhibits.

name: Sutro Baths Historic Ruins
description: Haunting remains of 1896 six-pool saltwater bath complex overlooking Ocean Beach and Marin Headlands; atmospheric clifftop exploration.
address: 1004 Point Lobos Avenue, San Francisco, CA 94121

name: Tamarine
description: Modern Vietnamese restaurant with silky pho, crispy banh mi, and craft cocktails. Sleek industrial interiors with an open kitchen showcasing traditional techniques and seasonal ingredients.

name: Tartine Bakery & Bread Experience
description: Iconic 20-year-old Mission Institution known for sourdough, morning pastries, and legendary wait lines; cult following for quality baked goods.

name: Tartine Manufactory Chocolate & Pastry
description: Industrial loft expansion featuring chocolate bar, espresso lab, and open-kitchen pastries; 2-story soaring ceilings in the central manufacturing facility.

name: The Tech Interactive
description: Hands-on science and technology museum with immersive exhibits on AI, robotics, and innovation. Perfect for curious minds of all ages.
address: 201 S Market St, San Jose, CA 95113

name: The Tech Interactive Store
description: Curated gift shop within The Tech museum featuring STEM-focused books, science kits, tech gadgets, and educational toys for all ages.
address: 201 S Market St, San Jose, CA 95113

name: The French Laundry
description: Michelin-starred culinary temple in Yountville helmed by Chef Thomas Keller, delivering France-inspired tasting menus of extraordinary technical precision and refined flavors. Iconic institution and destination dining experience.

name: Thomas Fogarty Winery
description: Santa Cruz Mountains treasure perched at 1,500ft with stunning Bay Area vistas and acclaimed Pinot Noir productions. Modern tasting room offers sophisticated wine experiences surrounded by redwoods and wildflowers.

name: Town & Country Village
description: Charming 1960s-era open-air shopping village with independent boutiques, gourmet markets, and local restaurants. Pedestrian paths, abundant parking, and community events.
address: 855 El Camino Real, Palo Alto, CA 94301

name: Twin Peaks Summit Hike
description: Short, steep climb to SF's second-highest point offering 360-degree city and bay views; marked trails with benches and clear weather vistas.
address: Twin Peaks Boulevard, San Francisco, CA 94114

name: University Avenue Shopping (Palo Alto)
description: Tree-lined pedestrian street with flagship boutiques, galleries, bookstores, and craft retailers. Historic architecture from the 1920s-40s mixed with contemporary storefronts.

name: V. Sattui Winery
description: Family-owned Napa winery featuring Italian-inspired wines and an exceptional gourmet deli perfect for picnicking among vineyard views. Historic stone buildings create an enchanting Old World atmosphere without tasting fees.

name: Winchester Mystery House
description: Bizarre Victorian mansion with 160 rooms, secret passages, and staircases to nowhere. A bizarre architectural wonder and historic landmark.
address: 525 S Winchester Blvd, San Jose, CA 95128

name: Windy Hill Preserve
description: Open grassland ridgeline with 360-degree valley and bay views from 1,900 feet elevation. Sweeping trails offering stunning panoramas and wildflowers in spring.
address: Portola State Road, Palo Alto, CA 94301

name: Wunderlich Park
description: Historic estate with giant redwoods, creek canyons, and 10 miles of quiet trails. Old-growth forests and meadows perfect for escaping crowds while immersed in nature.
